Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-tooltip repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: with the click trigger enabled (`openOnClick`), clicking an anchor element is supposed to open its tooltip. The library implements this by listening for click events at the document level. Because that delegated listener runs during the event's bubbling phase, if the anchor's own click handler calls `event.stopPropagation()`, the click never bubbles up to the document, the library's delegated listener never runs, and the tooltip fails to open. The correct behavior is that clicking the anchor opens its tooltip even when the anchor stops propagation of the click event. Fix how/when the library registers its delegated click handling so that a propagation-stopping anchor still opens the tooltip. Fix the library source (under `src/`); do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: no root access is needed. Dependencies are already installed via `npm install -g yarn@1.22.22 && yarn install --ignore-scripts --ignore-engines`. Run tests with Jest, e.g. `node_modules/.bin/jest src/test/tooltip-interaction-behavior.spec.js`. The event-delegation logic lives under `src/components/Tooltip/`. Fix the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

When the event\\n * fires, we iterate through all registered handlers for that type and phase.\\n *\\n * This reduces document-level listeners from O(N x eventTypes) to O(eventTypes).\\n */\\n\\ntype Handler = (event: Event) => void\\n\\ntype HandlerSets = Map<boolean, Set<Handler>>\\n\\nconst handlersByType = new Map<string, HandlerSets>()\\n\\nfunction getOrCreateSet(eventType: string, capture: boolean): Set<Handler> {\\n  let handlerSets = handlersByType.get(eventType)\\n  if (!handlerSets) {\\n    handlerSets = new Map()\\n    handlersByType.set(eventType, handlerSets)\\n  }\\n\\n  let set = handlerSets.get(capture)\\n  if (!set) {\\n    set = new Set()\\n    handlerSets.set(capture, set)\\n    document.addEventListener(eventType, dispatch, capture)\\n  }\\n  return set\\n}\\n\\nfunction dispatch(event: Event): void {\\n  const handlerSets = handlersByType.get(event.type)\\n  const capture = event.eventPhase === Event.CAPTURING_PHASE\\n  const handlers = handlerSets?.get(capture)\\n  if (handlers) {\\n    // Safe to iterate directly — mutations (add/remove) only happen in\\n    // setup/cleanup, not during dispatch. Set iteration is stable for\\n    // entries that existed when iteration began.\\n    handlers.forEach((handler) => {\\n      handler(event)\\n    })\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Register a handler for a document-level event type.\\n * Returns an unsubscribe function.\\n */\\nexport function addDelegatedEventListener(\\n  eventType: string,\\n  handler: Handler,\\n  capture = false,\\n): () => void {\\n  const set = getOrCreateSet(eventType, capture)\\n  set.add(handler)\\n\\n  return () => {\\n    set.delete(handler)\\n    if (set.size === 0) {\\n      const handlerSets = handlersByType.get(eventType)\\n      handlerSets?.delete(capture)\\n      document.removeEventListener(eventType, dispatch, capture)\\n      if (handlerSets?.size === 0) {\\n        handlersByType.delete(eventType)\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Reset for testing purposes.\\n */\\nexport function resetEventDelegation(): void {\\n  handlersByType.forEach((handlerSets, eventType) => {\\n    handlerSets.forEach((_handlers, capture) => {\\n      document.removeEventListener(eventType, dispatch, capture)\\n    })\\n  })\\n  handlersByType.clear()\\n}\\n\"}"}]
